title: Population at high risk depends on both exposure and vulnerability
subtitle: Comparing countries with similar levels of exposure or vulnerability
source: World Bank Scorecard Indicator, Hill et al.
visdescription: The figure shows comparisons between countries, where the share of people at high risk differs greatly, despite having similar levels of exposure or vulnerability.
keyFactLabel: people are exposed to climate-related hazards and living in highly vulnerable conditions

scenes.text: [emphasis: Burkina Faso ]and [emphasis: Kenya ]have similar levels of [emphasis: vulnerability]. In both countries, around 9 in 10 people  are identified as vulnerable in at least one dimension.

But [emphasis: exposure ]to climate hazards is three times higher in [emphasis: Burkina Faso]. Combining the number of vulnerable and exposed people, only 1 in 4 people in [emphasis: Kenya ]are at high risk from climate hazards, compared with 3 in 4 people in [emphasis: Burkina Faso].
Similarly, in East Asia and Pacific, almost 9 in 10 people in the [emphasis: Philippines ]and [emphasis: Vietnam] are [emphasis: exposed ]to climate hazards.

But despite their similar [emphasis: exposure ]profiles, slightly more than half the population of the [emphasis: Philippines ]is at risk, compared to around 20 percent in [emphasis: Vietnam]. This is due to the differing levels of vulnerability. In the [emphasis: Philippines], 3 in 5 people are vulnerable, while  in [emphasis: Vietnam],it is 1 in 5 people.
